title: Deceptive Dead End: Plan Depth Matters

runtime.expected_outcome: With policy_depth=1 the greedy agent gets stuck in the dead end; at depth=4 it backs out and reaches the goal.

symbols.pi: policy = ordered sequence of D actions

audiences.kid: A short-sighted agent walks into a dead end because it can't see around the corner. A farther-sighted one backs out and finds the goal.
audiences.real: Greedy policies fail here: the goal is only reachable via a detour. Policy depth controls how far ahead the agent rolls out before committing.
audiences.equation: G(pi) sums per-step EFE over the policy's D actions. Short D can be blind to reversible costs; longer D reveals the detour as net-negative-G.
audiences.derivation: Under a factorised policy q(a_1, ..., a_D) = prod q(a_t), depth-D planning enumerates sequences of length D. Policy posterior pi* = softmax(-G) over the enumerated set.

orchestrate.objective: Demonstrate that plan depth determines whether the agent escapes a deceptive dead end.
orchestrate.role: Plan-depth tutor anchored in the deceptive_dead_end maze.
orchestrate.context: deceptive_dead_end 7x6 maze; vary policy_depth between 1 (fails) and 4 (succeeds).

credits: Parr, Pezzulo & Friston (2022), Active Inference, MIT Press (CC BY-NC-ND), Ch 4 & App B.
